name: Quentin Tarantino
description: The films, cast, and style of director Quentin Tarantino

questions.q: Which 1992 crime film was Quentin Tarantino's feature directorial debut?

source.notes: Shot for $1.2M; launched Tarantino's career.

questions.q: Which actor plays Mr. Pink in 'Reservoir Dogs'?

source.notes: Buscemi famously complains about the tipping policy in the opening scene.

questions.q: In the 'Kill Bill' films, who plays the yellow-jumpsuited protagonist 'The Bride'?

source.notes: Character also known as Beatrix Kiddo.

questions.q: The movie 'Pulp Fiction' is set in which city?

source.notes: Many scenes shot in the San Fernando Valley.

questions.q: Who plays the Bible-quoting hit-man Jules Winnfield in 'Pulp Fiction'?

source.notes: Nominated for Best Supporting Actor.

questions.q: How many parts does Quentin Tarantino's 'Kill Bill' consist of?

source.notes: Volume 1 released 2003; Volume 2 in 2004.

questions.q: Before becoming a filmmaker, what job did Quentin Tarantino famously hold in Los Angeles?

source.notes: Worked at Video Archives in Manhattan Beach, CA in the late 1980s.

questions.q: In what year was 'Pulp Fiction' released, winning the Palme d'Or at Cannes?

source.notes: Won the Oscar for Best Original Screenplay.

questions.q: Who plays Mia Wallace, the gangster's wife in 'Pulp Fiction'?

source.notes: Iconic scenes include the Jack Rabbit Slim's dance contest and the adrenaline shot.

questions.q: What was the heist target in 'Reservoir Dogs'?

source.notes: The actual heist is never shown on screen — only the aftermath.

questions.q: What signature clothing item do the criminals in 'Reservoir Dogs' all wear?

source.notes: With white shirts and skinny black ties.

questions.q: Which Austrian actor won Best Supporting Actor for playing Nazi Colonel Hans Landa in 'Inglourious Basterds'?

source.notes: Won a second Oscar for 'Django Unchained' (2012).

questions.q: During which war is 'Inglourious Basterds' set?

source.notes: Set in Nazi-occupied France; culminates in an attack on a Paris cinema.

questions.q: What is the nickname of Brad Pitt's character Lt. Aldo Raine in 'Inglourious Basterds'?

source.notes: Raine demands 100 Nazi scalps from each of his men.

questions.q: Tarantino wrote the screenplay for which notorious 1994 Oliver Stone film?

source.notes: Tarantino later disowned Stone's reworking of the script.

questions.q: Who plays 'The Wolf', a cleanup fixer, in 'Pulp Fiction'?

source.notes: Keitel also played Mr. White in 'Reservoir Dogs'.

questions.q: Which song do the thieves discuss at length in the opening of 'Reservoir Dogs'?

source.notes: Tarantino himself delivers the extended Madonna analysis.

questions.q: In 'Django Unchained', which actor plays bounty hunter Dr. King Schultz, Django's mentor?

source.notes: Waltz won his second Best Supporting Actor Oscar for this role.

questions.q: For which two of his films has Quentin Tarantino won Best Original Screenplay Oscars?

source.notes: Won 1995 (Pulp Fiction) and 2013 (Django Unchained).

questions.q: What was Quentin Tarantino's 2015 western, described as his 8th film?
choices: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood

source.notes: Shot in 70mm Ultra Panavision; score by Ennio Morricone.

questions.q: Robert De Niro appears in only one Tarantino film, playing Louis Gara. Which film is it?
choices: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood

source.notes: Jackie Brown (1997) is Tarantino's adaptation of Elmore Leonard's novel 'Rum Punch'.

questions.q: In 'Django Unchained', which letter in Django's name is silent?

source.notes: Django himself helpfully points this out in the film: 'The D is silent.'
